title: iris spuria subsp. halophila
intro: Iris spuria subsp. halophila, also known as the salt iris, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the iris family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Iris spuria subsp. halophila include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infections. In herbal preparations, the root of the salt iris is often used to make infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
